name: Richard Lewis
bio: Richard Lewis was an American stand-up comedian known for frantic, stream-of-consciousness monologues about his neuroses, anxiety, and self-doubt, delivered while dressed in all black, which earned him the nickname The Prince of Pain. A fixture of 1980s late-night television with frequent appearances on Letterman, he later played a fictionalized version of himself on Curb Your Enthusiasm.

timeline.ev: First of dozens of appearances on Late Night with David Letterman
Begins recurring role on Curb Your Enthusiasm
Dies in Los Angeles at age 76

accolades: Ranked No. 45 on Comedy Central 100 Greatest Stand-Ups of All Time (2004)
Longtime fixture of Letterman, Leno, and Conan late-night stand-up
